steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $\frac{x(x + 3)}{x + 1}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Factor the numerator
steps.content: Start with the difference of squares inside the numerator: $$x^3-9x=x(x^2-9)=x(x-3)(x+3).$$

steps.title: Factor the denominator
steps.content: The denominator factors as $$x^2-2x-3=(x-3)(x+1).$$ Since $x\gt 3$, $x-3\ne0$, so the common factor $x-3$ can be canceled.

steps.title: Match the simplified expression
steps.content:
After canceling, the expression is
$$\frac{x(x+3)}{x+1}$$
That matches Choice D — $\frac{x(x + 3)}{x + 1}$.
